data.validation_feedback:
here are some feedbacks:
- The questions provided are very general and not specifically tailored to the extracted job requirements.
- We need to reference the actual job’s key technologies, frameworks, and competencies (e.g., Python, Django/Flask, REST API design, AWS services, Docker/Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, performance tuning, testing frameworks).
- The output should reflect those specifics in each question, ideal answer, and follow-up probes.

here are the changes:
```json
{
  "interview_questions": [
    {
      "Round": "Technical Screening",
      "Question": "Describe your recent experience building RESTful APIs in Django (or Flask). Which features of the framework did you leverage and why?",
      "Competency Tested": "Web Frameworks / REST API",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "References Django REST Framework (serializers, viewsets, routers) or Flask Blueprints; explains authentication/authorization choices; mentions request validation and error handling.",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "How did you structure your URL endpoints and serializers?",
        "What strategy did you use for pagination and filtering?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Round": "Technical Screening",
      "Question": "Explain the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) in CPython. How does it affect concurrency, and what approaches have you used to work around it?",
      "Competency Tested": "Python Concurrency",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "Understands GIL; distinguishes threading vs multiprocessing; mentions async/await, multiprocessing module, or external task queues (e.g., Celery).",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "Can you give an example where you switched from threads to async code?",
        "How did you decide between multiprocessing and an external task queue?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Round": "Technical Screening",
      "Question": "Describe how you set up containerized deployments using Docker. What considerations did you make for configuration, layering, and security?",
      "Competency Tested": "Containerization / Docker",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "Mentions Dockerfile best practices (small base images, multi-stage builds); environment variables; secret management; reducing image size; scanning for vulnerabilities.",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "How do you handle environment-specific configuration?",
        "What steps do you take to secure your Docker images?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Round": "Technical Screening",
      "Question": "Explain your experience with AWS (e.g., EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda). Which services did you use for scalability and cost-optimization?",
      "Competency Tested": "Cloud Architecture",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "Describes use of auto-scaling groups, load balancers, serverless functions; cost monitoring with AWS Cost Explorer; data storage choices (S3 vs EFS vs RDS).",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "How did you design your VPC and subnets for security?",
        "What metrics did you monitor and how did you respond to alerts?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Round": "Technical Screening",
      "Question": "What testing frameworks and methodologies have you applied in your Python projects? Describe your approach to unit, integration, and end-to-end tests.",
      "Competency Tested": "Testing / Quality Assurance",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "Mentions pytest or unittest; fixtures and parameterization; mock/patch; CI integration; test coverage reporting; testing in CI/CD pipeline.",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "How do you test asynchronous code or external service calls?",
        "How do you measure and enforce code coverage?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Round": "Technical Deep Dive",
      "Question": "Given a microservice handling user requests, how would you design and implement caching to reduce database load? Which layer(s) would you cache?",
      "Competency Tested": "Performance & Caching",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "Discusses Redis or Memcached; selects in-memory vs distributed cache; cache key design; TTL, invalidation; cache-aside vs write-through strategies.",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "How do you handle stale data or cache stampede?",
        "What metrics would you track to measure cache effectiveness?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Round": "Technical Deep Dive",
      "Question": "Design a CI/CD pipeline for a Python web service deployed on Kubernetes. Include steps from code push to production rollout.",
      "Competency Tested": "CI/CD / DevOps",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "References Git branching strategy; build/test in Jenkins/GitHub Actions/GitLab CI; Docker image build and push; Helm charts or Kubernetes manifests; blue/green or canary deployments.",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "How do you roll back a failed deployment?",
        "What automated checks would you include before merging to main?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Round": "Technical Deep Dive",
      "Question": "A customer reports your service is slow under peak load. Walk me through your profiling and optimization process in Python.",
      "Competency Tested": "Debugging & Optimization",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "Mentions cProfile, line_profiler, or py-spy; identifies hotspots; algorithmic improvements; database query optimization; concurrency vs batching; caching.",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "How did you verify the fix under load?",
        "What trade-offs did you consider between complexity and performance gain?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Round": "Technical Deep Dive",
      "Question": "Explain how you would secure API endpoints handling sensitive user data. What layers of security would you implement?",
      "Competency Tested": "Security Best Practices",
      "Ideal Answer Indicators": "Describes HTTPS/TLS; OAuth2/JWT; role-based access control; input validation; rate limiting; logging/auditing; secrets management.",
      "Follow-up Probes": [
        "How do you rotate and store secrets?",
        "What steps do you take to detect and respond to security incidents?"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
